title: lobelia elongata
intro: Lobelia elongata, also known as Indian tobacco or pukeweed, is a flowering plant native to North America, characterized by its blue or white blooms and slender stems. The plant contains lobeline, a compound with medicinal properties, which has been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties. Lobelia elongata has been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and aiding in detoxification. Herbal preparations of Lobelia elongata, such as teas and tinctures, are often used to relieve coughs, congestion, and other respiratory symptoms.
